steps.prompt: A tangent touches at one point
steps.body:
A tangent is a line that touches a circle at exactly one point, called the point of contact, P.
The radius OP runs from the center O out to that point.
What angle do the tangent and the radius make where they meet?

steps.prompt: Where the tangent touches, the tangent and radius meet at…
steps.subtitle: Commit to an answer, then see why.

options.label: A sharp angle that changes as P moves.
It depends on the circle's size.

steps.commitNote: Prediction saved. See why on the next step.

feedback.correct: Right — the tangent is perpendicular to the radius.
feedback.incorrect: See the next step: the tangent is perpendicular to the radius.

steps.body: At the point of contact, a tangent is always perpendicular to the radius: the angle between them is exactly 90°. It does not matter where the point of contact is or how big the circle is.

The radius is the shortest line from the center to the tangent.
The shortest line from a point to a straight line is always the perpendicular one.
So the radius must meet the tangent at 90° — any other angle would dip inside and cross the circle twice, making it a secant, not a tangent.
A second line from P splits the 90° into two parts. Here it makes 40° with the tangent, so it makes 90° − 40° = 50° with the radius. The two parts always add to 90°.

problems.prompt: A line from the point of contact makes 40° with the tangent. What angle does it make with the radius?

feedback.incorrect: The tangent meets the radius at 90°, so the two parts add to 90°.

problems.strongHint: Tangent and radius meet at 90°, so subtract: 90° − 40°.

problems.prompt: A line from the point of contact makes 30° with the radius. What angle does it make with the tangent?

feedback.incorrect: The tangent meets the radius at 90°, so the two parts add to 90°.

problems.strongHint: Tangent and radius meet at 90°, so subtract: 90° − 30°.

problems.prompt: A line from the point of contact makes 65° with the tangent. What angle does it make with the radius?

feedback.incorrect: The tangent meets the radius at 90°, so the two parts add to 90°.

problems.strongHint: Tangent and radius meet at 90°, so subtract: 90° − 65°.

steps.subtitle: A line from the point of contact makes 55° with the radius. What angle does it make with the tangent?

feedback.incorrect: The tangent and radius meet at 90°, so the two parts add to 90°.
